title: carya poilanei
intro: Carya poilanei, also known as the Vietnamese hickory, is a species of deciduous tree in the family Juglandaceae, native to Vietnam. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which is used to treat various ailments. The bark of Carya poilanei is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bark is traditionally prepared into herbal tea, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
